# Ablo — Full Context for LLMs

> **The Collaboration Layer for AI and Humans.** Real-time sync and mesh
> coordination for multiplayer productivity tools. Agents and humans share
> the same state, presence, and intents.

This file is a single fetch for AI coding assistants that need complete
integration context. Sections are ordered: concepts → quickstart →
per-subpath reference → architecture → troubleshooting.

## What the SDK does

- **Real-time sync** over WebSocket, backed by PostgreSQL + Redis pub/sub
- **Offline-first** IndexedDB persistence; optimistic mutations with automatic rollback
- **Schema-first** TypeScript modeling via Zod (`defineSchema`, `model`, `relation`, `mutable`, `readOnly`) — no codegen
- **Sync groups** for multi-party permission scoping (`org:{id}`, `team:{id}`, `deal:{id}`)
- **Capability-token auth** (Biscuit) for agent delegation with cryptographic attenuation
- **React hooks** — `useQuery`, `useOne`, `useMutate`, `useReader`, `useMutators`, `useUndoScope`, `usePresence`, `useIntent`. Zero-arg when the typed-global is declared.
- **Agents** — `new Ablo({ schema, capabilityToken })` where the token carries `participantKind: 'agent'` lifts presence/intents/snapshot to the top level. `ablo.<model>.perceive(id)` for per-request (AI SDK tool) short-lived views.
- **Mesh (`Ablo` class)** — `new Ablo({ schema })` + `ablo.<model>.join(id)` for per-matter participants with presence, intents, snapshots (stale-context protection). Browser flow via server-minted `capabilityToken`.

## Quickstart

### 1. Install

```bash
npm install @ablo/sync-engine
```

### 2. Declare the typed-global once

```ts
// src/ablo-sync.d.ts
import type { schema } from './sync/schema';

declare global {
  interface AbloSync {
    Schema: typeof schema;
    Presence: { cursor: { x: number; y: number } | null };
    Intents: { editTask: { taskId: string } };
    UserMeta: { id: string; email: string };
  }
}
export {};
```

### 3. Define the schema (Zod)

```ts
// src/sync/schema.ts
import { z, defineSchema, model, mutable, relation } from '@ablo/sync-engine/schema';

export const schema = defineSchema({
  projects: mutable.instant({
    name: z.string(),
    status: z.enum(['active', 'archived']).default('active'),
  }, { syncGroupFormat: 'project:{id}' }),

  tasks: mutable.instant(
    {
      title: z.string(),
      status: z.enum(['todo', 'doing', 'done']).default('todo'),
      priority: z.number().default(0),
      projectId: z.string().optional(),
    },
    {
      parent: schema.models.projects,
    },
  ),
}, { casing: 'snake_case' });
```

### 4. Wire the client + provider

```tsx
import { AbloProvider, useQuery, useMutate } from '@ablo/sync-engine/react';
import { schema } from './sync/schema';

function App({ userId, orgId, capabilityToken }) {
  return (
    <AbloProvider
      schema={schema}
      userId={userId}
      organizationId={orgId}
      capabilityToken={capabilityToken}
      // Optional: render during first bootstrap only. Defaults to
      // `<DefaultFallback />` (neutral spinner). Pass `null` for no
      // visual during boot, or `"passthrough"` to disable the gate.
      fallback={<AppSkeleton />}
    >
      <TaskList />
    </AbloProvider>
  );
}

// Components self-subscribe — no wrapper required. `useQuery` and
// `useOne` bridge their reactivity to React via `useSyncExternalStore`
// internally; plain function components re-render on sync changes.
function TaskList() {
  const tasks = useQuery('tasks', { where: { status: 'todo' } });
  const mutate = useMutate('tasks');
  return (
    <ul>
      {tasks.map(t => (
        <li key={t.id} onClick={() => mutate.update({ id: t.id, status: 'done' })}>
          {t.title}
        </li>
      ))}
      <button onClick={() => mutate.create({ title: 'New task' })}>Add</button>
    </ul>
  );
}
```

That's the full integration. Changes sync across clients in real-time. Works offline. Rolls back on server rejection.

---

## `@ablo/sync-engine/schema` — the DSL

### Exports

```ts
z                       // re-exported from zod
defineSchema            // wraps a model map into a typed Schema
model                   // model builder: (fields, relations?, options?)
relation                // belongsTo / hasMany / hasOne
mutable                 // intent-first sugar: mutable.lazy, mutable.instant
readOnly                // intent-first sugar: readOnly.lazy, readOnly.instant
field                   // advanced field-metadata helper (rarely needed)
indexed                 // wrap a Zod field to mark it indexed in the ObjectPool
query / defineQueries   // reusable parameterized read declarations
```

### Types exported

```ts
Schema, SchemaRecord, InferModel<S, K>, InferCreate<S, K>, InferModelNames<S>,
ModelDef, ModelOptions, LoadStrategy, PersistOptions, RelationRecord,
SugarOptions, DefineSchemaOptions, Casing, CasingConvention, CasingFn,
QueryDef, QueryRecord, Queries, InferQueryInput, InferQueryResult
```

### Field rules

- Fields are Zod schemas. Prefer `z.string()`, `z.number()`, `z.enum(['a','b'])`, `z.date()`, `z.boolean()`, `z.record(z.unknown())`, `z.object({...})`.
- Default values via `.default(value)` (Zod), NOT a second options argument.
- Optional via `.optional()`.
- Field names must round-trip through camelCase ↔ snake_case. `contentJSON` fails at schema-build; use `contentJson`.

### Intent-first sugar

```ts
mutable.instant(fields, options?)   // writable, bootstraps at connect
mutable.lazy(fields, options?)      // writable, fetched on first access per parent
readOnly.instant(fields, options?)  // read-only, bootstraps at connect
readOnly.lazy(fields, options?)     // read-only, fetched on demand
```

Options include `syncGroupFormat` (marks scopable entity), `parent` (cascade delete + sync-group derivation), `typename`, `tableName`, `bootstrapLimit`, `bootstrapOrderBy`.

### Base fields

Every model auto-includes: `id`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `organizationId?`, `createdBy?`. Don't declare these.

---

## Node usage — `new Ablo({ schema })`

```ts
import Ablo from '@ablo/sync-engine';

const ablo = new Ablo({ schema });  // reads ABLO_API_KEY; org from key

// Reads
const tasks = ablo.tasks.findMany({ where: { status: 'todo' }, orderBy: { priority: 'desc' } });
const task  = ablo.tasks.findById(taskId);
const count = ablo.tasks.count({ where: { status: 'todo' } });

// Writes (optimistic, queued offline)
const created = await ablo.tasks.create({ title: 'Ship it' });
await ablo.tasks.update(created.id, { status: 'done' });
await ablo.tasks.delete(created.id);
await ablo.tasks.archive(created.id);

// Subscriptions (outside React)
const unsub = ablo.tasks.subscribe(
  (task, delta) => console.log(task.id, delta.actionType),
  { where: { status: 'todo' } },
);

// Connection status
ablo.status.state
// 'syncing' | 'idle' | 'error' | 'offline' | 'reconnecting'
```

### The optimistic-first contract

Mutations resolve when **queued locally**, not when the server confirms. The UI updates instantly. If the server rejects the mutation later, the ObjectPool rolls back automatically. Watch `ablo.status` to detect rollbacks.

---

## `@ablo/sync-engine/react` — hooks

### Exports

```ts
AbloProvider, SyncGroupProvider, ClientSideSuspense, DefaultFallback
useQuery(modelKey, options?)
useOne(modelKey, id?)
useMutate(modelKey)
useReader(modelKey)
useMutators(mutators, options?)
useUndoScope(name, options?)
useParticipant(options?)
usePresence()
useIntent(intentName)
useSyncStatus()
useErrorListener(cb)
useCurrentUserId()
ModelScope                           // { live, archived, all }
```

All hooks infer types from `AbloSync['Schema']`. Components do NOT need to be wrapped — `useQuery` / `useOne` self-subscribe via `useSyncExternalStore`; plain function components re-render automatically on sync changes.

### Hook signatures (typed-global form)

```tsx
const tasks   = useQuery('tasks', {
  where: { status: 'todo', projectId: 'p1' },  // AND'd field-match filter
  filter: (t) => t.priority > 3,                // predicate (applied after `where`)
  orderBy: 'createdAt',
  order: 'desc',
  limit: 20,
  offset: 0,
  scope: ModelScope.live,
});                                             // Task[]

const task    = useOne('tasks', id);            // Task | undefined

const mutate  = useMutate('tasks');
await mutate.create({ title: 'Ship' });
await mutate.update({ id, status: 'done' });
await mutate.delete(id);
await mutate.createMany([{...}, {...}]);
await mutate.archive(id);

const read    = useReader('tasks');             // imperative snapshots (no subscribe)
read.findMany({ where: ... });
read.findById(id);
read.findFirst({ where: ... });
read.count({ where: ... });

const presence = usePresence();                 // your declared Presence shape
const edit     = useIntent('editTask');         // typed invoker for named intent
edit({ taskId });                               // arg typed against Intents['editTask']
```

### Provider wiring

```tsx
<AbloProvider
  schema={schema}
  userId={userId}
  organizationId={orgId}
  capabilityToken={capabilityToken}
  fallback={<AppSkeleton />}                       // optional — default <DefaultFallback />
  postBootstrap={[prefetchA, prefetchB]}           // optional
  onSessionExpired={() => router.replace('/signin')}
  onError={(err) => Sentry.captureException(err)}
  resolveUsers={({ userIds }) => fetchUsers(userIds)}
>
  <App />
</AbloProvider>
```

`<AbloProvider>` owns the full lifecycle: singleton rotation on auth change, Strict-Mode cleanup, `beforeunload`, session-expiry IndexedDB wipe, post-bootstrap hooks, mesh-client construction, AND first-bootstrap gating via the `fallback` prop. `useParticipant()` / `usePresence()` / `useIntent()` are available downstream without extra context wiring.

The `fallback` renders during the *first* bootstrap pass only — once the engine reaches `connected` the gate latches open and subsequent transient `connecting` / `needs-auth` states render `children` (the app's own UI handles those). Pass `fallback={null}` for no loading visual, or `fallback="passthrough"` to disable the gate entirely (debug helpers, error boundaries, analytics that must mount pre-ready). `<ClientSideSuspense>` is still exported for **nested** gating inside an already-ready provider — use it when app chrome should render immediately while a heavy subtree (e.g. a canvas) waits for its own query.

---

## Agents — `new Ablo({ schema, capabilityToken })`

An agent is an `Ablo` instance whose capability token declares `participantKind: 'agent'`. Participant primitives (`presence`, `intents`, `snapshot`) lift to the top level so there's no `.join()` ceremony for long-lived workers.

### Long-lived worker

```ts
import Ablo from '@ablo/sync-engine';

const ablo = new Ablo({
  schema,
  capabilityToken: process.env.ABLO_CAPABILITY_TOKEN!,
  syncGroups: ['org:default'],
});

ablo.tasks.subscribe(
  async (task, delta) => {
    if (delta.actionType !== 'I' && delta.actionType !== 'U') return;

    await using work = ablo.intents.writing(['Task', task.id], { ttl: '30s' });
    await ablo.tasks.update(task.id, { status: 'approved' });
    // Intent auto-releases on scope exit, whether the update succeeds or throws.
  },
  { where: { status: 'pending_review' } },
);
```

Mutations carry `createdBy: { kind: 'agent', id: <from token> }` in the delta log — the token, not the consumer, supplies identity. Model keys are lowercase (`'tasks'`), not capitalized.

### Per-request (AI SDK tool call)

Short-lived snapshot-and-go view, no persistent WebSocket:

```ts
const view = await ablo.matters.perceive(matterId);
const answer = await yourLLM.ask({ context: view.data, signal: view.signal });
await view.clauses.update(id, { body: answer }, { readAt: view.stamp });
```

`view` auto-disposes. See `docs/agent.md`.

---

## `@ablo/sync-engine` (0.2.0) — the `Ablo` class

`new Ablo({ schema })` returns a client with capability tokens, presence, intents, and watermarks. Humans and AI agents are equal participants on the same live stream. Tools, skills, prompts, models stay in the customer's code.

### Construction

```ts
import Ablo, { session, agent } from '@ablo/sync-engine';
import { schema } from './schema';

// Reads ABLO_API_KEY from env. Org is derived server-side from the
// API key — `organizationId` is NOT required.
const ablo = new Ablo({ schema });

// Browser variant: pre-minted capability token from your server.
// No ABLO_API_KEY, no session cookies, no allowed-origins needed.
const ablo = new Ablo({
  schema,
  capabilityToken: tokenFromServer,
  onTokenRefresh: async () => (await fetch('/api/ablo/token').then((r) => r.json())).token,
});

// Functional alias; same result.
import { createMesh } from '@ablo/sync-engine';
const ablo = createMesh({ schema });
```

### Three integration shapes

1. **Server agent:** `new Ablo({ schema })` + `ablo.matters.join(id)`. Reads env.
2. **Browser:** server mints via `ablo.admin.capabilities.create({ allowedSyncGroups, ttlSeconds })`, browser holds `capabilityToken`.
3. **Sub-agent:** `parent.join(childAgent, { scope })` — attenuates parent's capability.

Runnable examples live in [`examples/`](examples/).

### Joining

```ts
// Model-scoped shortcut — the 90% path.
const p = await ablo.matters.join(matterId, {
  label: 'DD Bot',           // human-readable; peers see it in presence
  ttlSeconds: '24h',          // accepts number (seconds) or duration string
  // id auto-generated as agent_<uuid>; pass agent: { id, label } only
  // when you need a stable participant id across restarts
});
// Auto-connected — `p` is live when promise resolves. `autoConnect: false` to opt out.
// `participant.autoRefresh()` rotates the token before expiry.

// Generic escape hatch for multi-entity scopes or bring-your-own-agent.
const p = await ablo.join(myAgentObject, {
  scope: { matters: matterId, teams: [t1, t2] },
  as: session({ id, userId, organizationId }), // plain-English alias for onBehalfOf
});
```

### Coordination (every participant, always on)

```ts
// Presence — advisory "who's doing what".
p.presence.editing(['Clause', id]);        // verb methods
p.presence.viewing(['Matter', matterId]);
p.presence.idle();
p.presence.update({ entityType, entityId, action: 'custom' });  // escape hatch
for await (const peers of p.presence) { /* reactive roster */ }

// Intents — claim a write; peers yield. await using auto-revokes.
await using work = p.intents.writing(['Clause', id], { ttl: '3m' });
await using work = p.intents.editing(['Clause', id]);

// Snapshots — stale-context protection. Typed via InferModel, not `unknown`.
const snap = await p.snapshot({ clauses: [id] });
snap.clauses[id].text        // typed field from schema
snap.stamp                   // opaque version marker; pass as readAt on writes
snap.signal                  // AbortSignal — fires on captured-entity deltas
snap.onChange((c) => { ... }) // callback form

// Deltas — firehose of mutations.
p.onDelta((delta) => { ... });
for await (const delta of p.deltas) { ... }
```

### Mutations (typed via InferCreate / InferModel)

```ts
await p.create('documents', [{ title: 'Memo' }]);       // ← data typed from schema
await p.update('documents', [{ id, title: 'Memo v2' }]);
await p.del('documents', [id]);
await p.archive('documents', [id]);
await p.unarchive('documents', [id]);
```

### Admin resources

```ts
ablo.admin.capabilities.create({ allowedSyncGroups, ttlSeconds }) / .del(id)
ablo.admin.roles.create({ name, read, write }) / .retrieve(id) / .list() / .update(id, ...) / .del(id)
ablo.admin.members.create({ userId, organizationId, roleId, scope })
ablo.admin.audit.list({ principal, limit })
```

Server endpoints for `roles`, `members`, `audit` are on the migration roadmap — methods throw `AbloError(mesh_not_implemented)` when the endpoint isn't live. The SDK surface is stable. `capabilities` is fully live and is the server-side mint path for Shape 2 (browser).

---

## Authentication (hosted-server concern)

Clients get capability tokens from the hosted server via `ablo.join(...)` / `ablo.<model>.join(...)` / `ablo.capabilities.create(...)`. The client never mints tokens locally — Biscuit minting, attenuation, and verification all live on the server. SDK consumers just pass the received token as a bearer on subsequent requests.

- **API keys** (`sk_live_*`, `sk_test_*`) — server-to-server auth for customer backends
- **Biscuit capability tokens** — cryptographically attenuable, short-lived scoped tokens, minted by the hosted server on `POST /api/auth/capability`
- **Session cookies** (Better Auth) — human users via browser

Caveat vocabulary includes `sync_group`, `operation` (`create|update|delete|archive`), `ttl`, and the participant kind (`agent|human`). See `docs/auth.md` for the full flow.

---

## `@ablo/core` — shared error hierarchy

Companion package, ~15 KB. Depended on as a peer by `@ablo/sync-engine` and by the hosted server (`apps/sync-server`). Contains the typed error classes — `AbloError`, `AbloValidationError`, `AbloPermissionError`, `CapabilityError`, `AbloStaleContextError`, `AbloIdempotencyError`, `translateHttpError`, etc.

SDK consumers keep importing errors from `@ablo/sync-engine` (the main barrel re-exports everything from `@ablo/core`). Non-SDK hosts (agent workers, CLI tools, server runtimes) depend on `@ablo/core` directly so they don't pull in the client WebSocket + IndexedDB graph just to discriminate an error.

---

## `@ablo/sync-engine` (main barrel)

```ts
Ablo (default export — the canonical client)
defineSchema,
defineMutators, UndoManager,
postQuery,
AbloError, AbloValidationError, AbloPermissionError,
```

Custom mutators:

```ts
import { defineMutators } from '@ablo/sync-engine';

export const mutators = defineMutators(schema, {
  tasks: {
    async complete({ tx, args }: { tx; args: { id: string } }) {
      const task = tx.tasks.findById(args.id);
      if (!task) throw new Error('not found');
      await tx.tasks.update(task.id, { status: 'done', completedAt: new Date() });
    },
  },
});
```

---

## Architecture (one-paragraph)

The delta log (`sync_deltas` table) is the source of truth — append-only. Every mutation produces one delta tagged with a monotonic `syncId`, participant typing (`createdBy: { kind, id }`), and sync-group scope. Clients receive deltas via WebSocket and apply them to an in-memory MobX ObjectPool; IndexedDB persistence gives offline durability. Bootstrap delivers the initial snapshot at connect. Ghost removal purges local entities not in the server's view, so stale optimistic state can't linger. The server is Go (sync-engine) with PostgreSQL (Neon) + Redis pub/sub for cross-instance delta fan-out.

## Requirements

- Node.js 22.0.0+ (Web Crypto as globals)
- TypeScript 5.0+ for schema/mutator inference
- Browsers: ES2022 (Safari 16+, Chrome 110+, Firefox 110+)

## Errors

- `AbloError` — base class; all SDK errors extend this
- `AbloValidationError` — input/shape errors (wrong scope, missing schema ref, invalid TTL)
- `AbloPermissionError` — capability rejected by server
- HTTP errors translated via `translateHttpError(status, body, requestId)` — `requestId` carries the `x-request-id` header for support correlation

## Common gotchas

- Field names must be camelCase (single-capitalization). `contentJSON` fails; use `contentJson`.
- Model keys at call sites are lowercase (`useQuery('tasks', ...)`, `agent.on('tasks', ...)`) — they match the schema object keys.
- `ablo.join` / `ablo.<model>.join` requires `scope` when `as` (or the legacy `onBehalfOf`) is set under the default strict delegation policy. Pass `scope` explicitly or opt into `delegationPolicy: 'permissive'` on construction.
- Sub-agent spawn via `participant.join(child, opts)` always hits the mint endpoint even when the parent was constructed with `capabilityToken` — attenuation requires a server-side mint. Top-level `ablo.join(...)` on a capability-token client skips the mint (the token IS the capability).
- `defineMutators` handlers run against a `tx` object with the same shape as `sync` — read/write via `tx.tasks.findById(...)`, `tx.tasks.update(...)`.
- `await sync.ready()` before the first query. Otherwise `findMany` returns `[]` until bootstrap finishes.
- `withSync` is **no longer required** — kept only as a deprecated alias of `mobx-react-lite`'s `observer` for back-compat with older code. `useQuery` / `useOne` self-subscribe via `useSyncExternalStore`; plain function components re-render on sync changes.
